You are a CLEAN-ROOM implementer under the campaign protocol at /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md — read it first and comply strictly.
ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source (*.hs) of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk (notably /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/{lib,src}/ and /home/kamilner/tamarin-prover-testing/{lib,src}/). No web access of any kind. You MAY: run the reference binary/servers via your cluster's oracle scripts (export PATH="$PATH:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin" if invoking directly — maude/dot live there), read .spthy example files, and read/write freely inside your own cluster directory under /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/. Do not modify anything outside your cluster directory. Do not run git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction (command + salient output) by appending to your cluster's workspace QUERIES.log; record every derived behavioral fact in BEHAVIOR.md with provenance (which probe established it); write your round summary into a REPORT file in the workspace (append a new round section). Code comments must describe current behavior only — no narration of changes or process. If your model's training data tempts you to "recall" how tamarin's internals work, do not use it: every behavioral claim must trace to a logged probe or a captured fixture. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary at the end (no structured output).

UNIT A (web dispatch), round 4 — extend Route::parse and Server<P: ProverOps> to the FULL route surface so the dispatcher can own the consumer's entire request path. Live-server ports: use 3100-3199 only.
Cluster: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/ — read workspace BEHAVIOR.md, QUERIES.log, the route census and captured envelope manifests first; live reference server recipe in your cluster. Crate: workspace/web-clean.

Your dispatch currently covers main/*, overview/*, autoprove, next/prev, source/message, intdot, interactive-graph-def, and the edit POST. Extend to the REST of the observed surface: the root page (GET /) and theory-upload POST, /static/* asset serving, download, reload, kill, the equiv overview variant, del/path, verify, and robots.txt. For each: probe live and/or from your captured manifests — methods, status codes, headers (content-type, disposition, caching), redirect behavior, and body bytes; byte-parity tests per route family.

State ownership: Server must own ALL version/theory state for the full surface — one state machine. Extend your version-map/counter semantics per observed behavior across upload, reload, kill, delete sequences (probe the index/version lifecycles live: what appears on the root page after each operation, which indices are reused or retired, what 404s after kill/delete).

Grow the ProverOps trait minimally for what the new routes need — pure data/fragment producers only; keep all transport concerns in Server. All existing tests stay green. Stop all servers when done.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

1 # BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec of the tamarin-prover interactive web UI 2 3 Derived entirely from black-box observation: 81 crawl manifests in 4 `oracle/captured_responses/` (captured OUTPUT) plus live probing of the 5 sanctioned server (`QUERIES.log` [L0]–[L4]). No prover source was read. 6 7 Terms: **web layer** = the code that dispatches routes, escapes, and wraps 8 strings into envelopes/pages. **prover fragment** = a string the prover produced 9 (pretty-printed terms, constraint systems, method names, DOT bodies) that the 10 web layer embeds opaquely. This spec pins down the web layer; prover fragments 11 are treated as inputs. 12 13 --- 14 15 ## 1. Route grammar 16 17 Every dynamic route has the shape: 18 19 ``` 20 /thy//// 21 ``` 22 23 * `` — analysis kind. Only `trace` observed (a `diff` kind is 24 plausible but never appears in the corpus). 25 * `` — theory **version** selector. In real requests it is a decimal 26 number. The manifests store it as the literal token `#`; live probing shows 27 `%23` returns **404**, so `#` is the crawl tool's placeholder meaning "the 28 server's current version", and every URL the server *emits* uses the resolved 29 numeric index. Applying a proof method creates a new version, so the index 30 seen in a response's links is generally larger than the one requested. 31 * `` — selects the response family: 32 33 | handler | body kind | notes | 34 |-----------------------------|-----------|-------| 35 | `main/…` | JSON | AJAX content pane; envelope `{html,title}` or `{redirect}` | 36 | `overview/…` | HTML | full theory-view page (see §5) | 37 | `intdot/…` | HTML | graph mini-page embedding `` (§8) | 38 | `interactive-graph-def/…` | DOT | Graphviz source for a proof node (§8) | 39 | `next/…`, `prev/…` | text | a single navigation URL (§9) | 40 | `autoprove/…` | JSON | `{redirect}`; **text** with HTTP status 0 on timeout | 41 | `source`, `message` | text | the theory source, verbatim (§9) | 42 43 `main/*` sub-handlers (observed, exhaustive over the corpus): 44 45 | sub-route | meaning | 46 |-----------|---------| 47 | `main/help` | theory header + wellformedness report + keyboard help | 48 | `main/message` | signature + construction/deconstruction rules | 49 | `main/rules` | multiset rewriting rules (+ restrictions) | 50 | `main/tactic` | tactic(s) listing | 51 | `main/cases/{raw\|refined}/{level}/{n}` | source cases | 52 | `main/lemma/{name}` | lemma summary | 53 | `main/proof/{lemma}/{path…}` | constraint system + applicable methods at a proof node; `path` is a `/`-joined sequence of case-name segments, root `_` | 54 | `main/method/{lemma}/{n}` | apply method `n` → `{redirect}` | 55 | `main/add/{pos}` | add-lemma form (`pos` = a lemma name or `` ⇒ `%3Cfirst%3E`) | 56 | `main/edit/{name}` | edit-lemma form | 57 | `main/delete/{name}` | delete-lemma confirmation | 58 59 `overview/*` mirrors `main/*` for the pages that render a full shell: 60 `overview/help`, `overview/proof/{lemma}/{path…}`. `intdot/*` and 61 `interactive-graph-def/*` take the same `proof/{lemma}/{path…}` (and 62 `cases/…`) tails. 63 64 ### Authoritative handler/kind census (all 81 manifests, 48824 dynamic routes) 65 66 Enumerated with a template pass ([Q020]–[Q021]); the response `kind` is fixed 67 per handler: 68 69 | handler | kind | count | argument shape | 70 |--------------------------|-------------|-------|----------------| 71 | `main` | json | 17609 | see `main/*` table above | 72 | `interactive-graph-def` | dot | 14705 | `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 73 | `intdot` | html | 14705 | `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 74 | `overview` | html | 473 | `help` \| `proof/{lemma}[/{path…}]` | 75 | `next` | text | 392 | `normal/proof/{lemma}` | 76 | `prev` | text | 392 | `normal/proof/{lemma}` | 77 | `autoprove` | json (text on timeout) | 392 | `idfs/{bound}/False/proof/{lemma}` | 78 | `source` | text | 81 | (none) | 79 | `message` | text | 81 | (none) | 80 81 `autoprove/{strategy}/{bound}/{allSol}/proof/{lemma}[/path…]` is the full 82 autoprove shape (see §13.2). It answers with a `{redirect}` to the resolved 83 `overview/proof/{lemma}` at a **new** version index — live probe [L6]/[L9] show 84 `autoprove/idfs/0/False/proof/types` on a version-1 theory returning 85 `{"redirect":"/thy/trace/2/overview/proof/nonce_secrecy"}`. The 6 corpus 86 "status 0" entries carry the body `REQUEST_ERROR: TimeoutError('timed out')`, 87 which is the **crawler's own** timeout string (the client gave up while the 88 prover ran), not a web-layer response family ([Q035] — corrects the earlier note). 89 90 `next`/`prev` are pure text: a single navigation URL. The target need not be a 91 proof node — live probe shows `prev/normal/proof/types` (the first lemma) 92 returning `/thy/trace/1/main/cases/refined/0/0`. 93 94 Content-types (live probe [L6]): json = `application/json; charset=utf-8`, 95 text = `text/plain; charset=utf-8`, html = `text/html; charset=utf-8`. 96 97 Static/other routes seen in emitted links (not in the crawl of dynamic content): 98 `GET /` (index), `POST /` (upload), `POST /thy/…/reload`, 99 `GET /thy/…/download/{file}`, `POST /thy/…/get_and_append/{file}`, 100 `GET /thy/…/edit/{edit|add|delete}/{name}` (form POST targets), 101 `/static/**`, `/static/LICENSE`, `/static/img/tamarin-logo-3-0-0.png`. 102 103 Unmatched paths → **404** full HTML page (§7). 104 105 --- 106 107 ## 2. Response envelopes 108 109 ### JSON (`main/*`, `method/*`, `autoprove/*`) 110 Exactly two compact shapes, keys in this order, **no** insignificant whitespace, 111 **no** trailing newline: 112 113 ``` 114 {"html":,"title":} — content pane 115 {"redirect":} — client should navigate to the URL 116 ``` 117 118 JSON string escaping is standard: only `"` `\` and control chars are escaped; 119 `/ < > &` are **not** escaped; non-ASCII is emitted as **literal UTF-8** (no 120 `\uXXXX`). `serde_json`'s default output reproduces this byte-for-byte 121 (verified on 2450 distinct bodies). 122 123 ### HTML (`overview/*`, `intdot/*`, `/`, 404) 124 `text/html; charset=utf-8`. Full documents beginning `\n…` 125 and ending `` with **no** trailing newline. 126 127 ### text (`source`, `message`, `next`, `prev`) 128 `text/plain`. Returned verbatim; `source`/`message` end at the theory's final 129 `end` with no trailing newline; `next`/`prev` are a bare URL path. 130 131 ### DOT (`interactive-graph-def/*`) 132 Graphviz source; the empty-graph skeleton ends with `}\n`. 133 134 --- 135 136 ## 3. Escaping rules 137 138 The web layer's HTML text escape maps five characters and passes everything else 139 (including all prover-emitted unicode operators) through unchanged: 140 141 ``` 142 & -> & 143 < -> < 144 > -> > 145 " -> " 146 ' -> ' 147 ``` 148 149 Applied to: the "loaded from" path, lemma names/text in the edit form, the add 150 form's position marker (`` → `<first>` in the form action), the 151 echoed path in the 404 page. Each mapping was witnessed in a capture. Prover 152 fragments arrive already escaped (e.g. pair terms show `< … >`), so the 153 web layer does not double-escape them. 154 155 URL segments are percent-encoded independently: `` appears as 156 `%3Cfirst%3E` in hrefs. 157 158 --- 159 160 ## 4. What is web-layer vs prover-produced 161 162 Web layer (reproduced by `web-clean`): route dispatch; the JSON envelopes; the 163 page shell / index / 404 / intdot templates; the proof-script pane scaffolding 164 (theory header link, item links, add/edit/delete links, `by sorry`, blank-line 165 spacing, `end`); the proof-tree **line** markup (indentation, anchors, 166 `case`/`next`/`qed`/`by` keywords); the form bodies; HTML escaping. 167 168 Prover fragments (supplied as opaque inputs): pretty-printed signatures, rules, 169 lemma formulas, constraint systems, applicable-method names, source cases, 170 proof-method texts, non-empty DOT graphs, the theory source string, wf-warning 171 text, the rules label/count and sources descriptions. 172 173 --- 174 175 ## 5. Full theory-view page (`overview/*`) 176 177 Fully determined by six values: `NAME`, `IDX` (numeric), `VERSION`, `FILENAME`, 178 the west-pane inner HTML, and the center-pane inner HTML. After substituting 179 `IDX`/`NAME`/`FILENAME`/`VERSION`, the scaffolding is byte-identical across 180 theories. Layout: 181 182 ``` 183 \nTheory: NAME …fixed link/script set… 184

1	# REPORT3.md — handler semantics (the interactive-UI state machine)
2	
3	Round 3 of the `weblayer` clean-room cluster. Prior rounds reproduced the
4	response **bodies** (JSON envelopes, page shells, proof-script pane, forms, DOT,
5	404) byte-for-byte. This round adds the **behaviour**: what each route *returns*
6	and how the theory-version state evolves — the UI state machine — parameterised
7	over a `ProverOps` callback trait the ported prover supplies at integration.
8	
9	Everything below is derived from black-box observation only: the 81 crawl
10	manifests in `oracle/captured_responses/` (captured OUTPUT) and live probing of
11	the sanctioned oracle binary ([L7]-[L16], Tutorial.spthy, ports 3137-3141). No
12	file under `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` was read; the only tamarin-rs touch was
13	executing the sanctioned binary (incl. `interactive --help` and
14	`--interface=*4`, extra CLI flags the protocol permits).
15	
16	## Deliverables
17	
18	* `src/dispatch.rs` (new) — the `Server<T: ProverOps>` state machine:
19	  route dispatch, theory-version management, response-envelope assembly.
20	* `src/route.rs` — grammar refined: `main/method/{lemma}/{n}[/path...]` now carries
21	  the proof path (the number precedes it); structured parsers `Autoprove`, `Nav`,
22	  `OverviewView`, `EditVerb` for the dispatcher.
23	* `src/forms.rs` — `edit_rows` implements the real textarea `rows=` formula
24	  (blocker b), replacing the round-1 hardcoded `rows="8"`.
25	* `tests/dispatch.rs` (15 tests) + `tests/fixtures/r3_*` — capture-backed
26	  behaviour/byte-parity tests.
27	* `BEHAVIOR.md` section 13, `QUERIES.log` [Q029]-[L16].
28	
29	`cargo test` -> **55 passing** (21 unit + 15 dispatch + 19 parity), `cargo clippy
30	--tests --examples` clean.
31	
32	## The state machine (`ProverOps` + `Server`)
33	
34	The web layer owns exactly two pieces of state: a `BTreeMap<u64, Theory>` of
35	theory versions and a monotonic `next_index`. `ProverOps` is the prover boundary
36	- parse/edit/add/delete a lemma, apply a proof method at a path, autoprove, the
37	next/prev traversal target, and the opaque pretty-printed fragments (west pane,
38	center content, lemma source, theory source, DOT). `Server::dispatch(req)` parses
39	the route, resolves the numeric index, and makes every web-layer decision:
40	
41	### 1. Version increments (spec item 1)
42	* **Proof ops** (`main/method`, `autoprove`) allocate a **fresh** index
43	  `= (max ever allocated)+1` and leave the base version intact; every version
44	  stays resolvable ([L8],[L9],[L11]).
45	* **Structural edits** (POST `edit/{edit,add,delete}`) mutate the theory **in
46	  place** at the requested index - no new version ([L12]).
47	* Navigation/views never change the version set ([L10]).
48	
49	Byte evidence: `GET main/method/Client_session_key_secrecy/1` on v1 ->
50	`{"redirect":"/thy/trace/2/overview/proof/Client_session_key_secrecy/_"}`
51	(identical to capture `r3_method_redirect.json`); a second identical request ->
52	`/thy/trace/3/...`; `POST edit/delete/Client_auth` -> `303`, no new index.
53	
54	### 2. autoprove variants (spec item 2)
55	Route `autoprove/{strategy}/{bound}/{allSol}/proof/{lemma}[/path...]`. The
56	keyboard-help "a/b/all/characterization" matrix ([Q031]) decodes as: `strategy` in
57	{`idfs` prove, `characterize`}; `bound` `0`=unbounded(`a`/`A`), `5`=bounded
58	(`b`/`B`); `allSol` `False`=stop-at-first(lowercase), `True`=all-solutions
59	("all", uppercase). Every variant answers `200` + JSON
60	`{"redirect":"/thy/trace/{new}/overview/proof/{lemma}/{focus}"}` at a new version
61	([L9]). `Autoprove::parse` recovers the full matrix from the URL (tested).
62	
63	### 3. proof-step / del / add (spec item 3)
64	* `main/method/{lemma}/{n}[/path...]` - number **before** the case-name path
65	  ([Q032]); `200` JSON redirect, new version.
66	* `POST edit/delete/{name}` -> `303` `Location: .../overview/help`, empty body, in
67	  place.
68	* `POST edit/edit/{name}` (`lemma-text`) -> success `303` `.../overview/edit/{name}`;
69	  **failure** `200` re-rendering the full edit-form page, theory unchanged
70	  ([L13]).
71	* `POST edit/add/{pos}` -> success `303` `.../overview/add/{pos}`; failure `200`
72	  add-form page. `overview/edit|add/...` are full pages that appear only as these
73	  POST-redirect targets.
74	
75	### 4. Traversal & path encoding (spec item 1)
76	`next|prev/{mode}/proof/{lemma}` -> `200` `text/plain` bare URL at the same
77	version (`nav_target` is a prover computation; `mode` opaque). Proof paths are the
78	raw `/`-join of case-name segments (root `_`), segments `[A-Za-z0-9_]`, **no
79	percent-encoding** ([Q034]).
80	
81	## Integration blockers
82	
83	### (b) edit-form textarea `rows=` - SOLVED
84	`rows = (#newlines in the raw lemma source) + 2`, verified over 4 lemmas
85	(9/11/7/10 -> 11/13/9/12, [L14]). Implemented as `forms::edit_rows`;
86	`edit_form_envelope_byte_parity_with_rows_formula` reproduces the whole captured
87	edit-form JSON envelope for two lemmas (rows 11 and 9) byte-for-byte.
88	
89	### (a) non-local page shell - NEGATIVE RESULT (environment-limited)
90	The spec expected the served shell to differ for a non-`127.0.0.1` client. I
91	bound the oracle to all IPv4 interfaces (`--interface=*4`) and fetched every route
92	BOTH via `127.0.0.1` and via the machine's non-loopback `212.100.173.110` - the
93	server access-log **confirms** the peer is `212.100.173.110`. Result: **every
94	route is byte-identical** (read views, forms, index, 404, and mutating GET/POST -
95	method/edit/delete/reload all succeed and match). Host and
96	`X-Forwarded-For`/`X-Real-IP` headers have no effect ([L15]).
97	
98	So in oracle **1.13.0** the shell is **origin-independent**. The locality
99	predicate evidently classifies all of the machine's own interface addresses
100	(loopback + `212.x`) as local, so a genuinely foreign peer cannot be synthesised
101	on a single host - the non-local shell's bytes are **unobservable in this
102	environment**. I therefore did *not* fabricate a variant from memory. The
103	architecture leaves a clean hook: the shell renderer can be parameterised over an
104	`origin_local` flag the ported prover drives; the exact non-local bytes remain a
105	documented gap pending a multi-host capture (or a version where the difference is
106	observable at the HTTP boundary).
107	
108	## Honesty / scope notes
109	
110	* `ProverOps` returns opaque fragments; the tests feed **canned** fragments taken
111	  from live captures (focus paths `_`, next/prev targets, lemma sources) so the
112	  asserted bytes are the **web-layer** assembly (envelope, status, Location,
113	  version index, URL template), never re-derived prover logic.
114	* Center pane of overview pages = the sibling `main/*` html + one trailing space
115	  (round-2 finding), wired through `center_for`.
116	* The `#`/current index does not resolve (a literal `#` is a URL fragment; `%23`
117	  404s), so `dispatch` treats a non-numeric/absent index as `404` ([L2]).
118	* Status-0 corpus autoprove entries are the crawler's `TimeoutError` string, not a
119	  server response family - corrected in BEHAVIOR section 1/13.2 ([Q035]).
120	* Not modelled (side-effecting file ops, seen only as link targets):
121	  `reload`, `download/{file}`, `get_and_append/{file}`, `POST /` upload. The
122	  `diff`/`equiv` theory-kind never appears.

1	//! The full-page theory-view HTML shell returned by the `overview/*` routes.
2	//!
3	//! Observed structure (constant across theories after substituting the four
4	//! parameters below): a fixed `<head>` of stylesheet/script links, a north
5	//! header bar, then four layout panes — west "Proof scripts", east "Debug
6	//! information" (always empty), and center "Visualization display". The west
7	//! pane embeds the proof-script markup (see [`crate::proofscript`]); the center
8	//! pane embeds the currently-selected main content HTML (the same HTML the
9	//! corresponding `main/*` route returns in its JSON `html` field).
10	//!
11	//! Scaffolding constants in `shell_template` are byte-exact copies of oracle
12	//! output with four `§`-delimited slots: NAME, IDX, VERSION, FILENAME. The
13	//! body has no trailing newline (ends `</html>`). All internal links use the
14	//! resolved numeric theory index, never `#`.
15	
16	use crate::escape::html_escape;
17	use crate::shell_template::{PAGE_MID, PAGE_PREFIX, PAGE_TAIL};
18	
19	/// Parameters that vary between rendered pages.
20	pub struct PageParams<'a> {
21	    /// Theory name, shown in `<title>Theory: NAME</title>`.
22	    pub theory_name: &'a str,
23	    /// Resolved numeric theory index used in every internal URL.
24	    pub index: u64,
25	    /// Tamarin version string shown in the header (e.g. `"1.13.0"`).
26	    pub version: &'a str,
27	    /// Source filename used in the download / append links (e.g. `"foo.spthy"`).
28	    pub filename: &'a str,
29	}
30	
31	/// Render the full theory-view page from the shell parameters plus the already
32	/// rendered west (proof-script) and center (main content) pane inner HTML.
33	pub fn render_page(p: &PageParams, west_inner: &str, center_inner: &str) -> String {
34	    let idx = p.index.to_string();
35	    let prefix = PAGE_PREFIX
36	        .replace("§NAME§", &html_escape(p.theory_name))
37	        .replace("§IDX§", &idx)
38	        .replace("§VERSION§", p.version)
39	        .replace("§FILENAME§", p.filename);
40	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(
41	        prefix.len() + west_inner.len() + PAGE_MID.len() + center_inner.len() + PAGE_TAIL.len(),
42	    );
43	    out.push_str(&prefix);
44	    out.push_str(west_inner);
45	    out.push_str(PAGE_MID);
46	    out.push_str(center_inner);
47	    out.push_str(PAGE_TAIL);
48	    out
49	}

1	//! The `intdot/*` mini-page and the `interactive-graph-def/*` DOT skeleton.
2	//!
3	//! `intdot/<path>` returns a tiny standalone HTML page whose only content is a
4	//! `<dot-graph-viz>` custom element pointing at the matching
5	//! `interactive-graph-def/<path>` DOT source (same trailing path, resolved
6	//! numeric index). No trailing newline (ends `</html>`).
7	//!
8	//! `interactive-graph-def/<path>` returns Graphviz DOT. For a proof node with
9	//! no constructed graph (e.g. a freshly opened lemma) the body is the fixed
10	//! empty-graph skeleton below, which DOES end in a newline. Non-empty graphs
11	//! are prover-produced and out of scope for this template.
12	
13	const INTDOT_TEMPLATE: &str = r#"<!DOCTYPE html>
14	<html><head><meta charset="UTF-8" /><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" /><title>Theory: §NAME§</title><style> body,html{width: 100%; height: 100%; overflow: hidden; margin: 0; padding: 0; }</style><link rel="stylesheet" href="/static/css/intdot-style.css"><script type="module" src="/static/js/intdot-graph.es.js"></script></script></head><body><dot-graph-viz dotsrc="§DOTSRC§"></dot-graph-viz>
15	</body></html>"#;
16	
17	/// The empty-graph DOT skeleton returned for a proof node with no graph.
18	pub const EMPTY_GRAPH_DOT: &str = "digraph \"G\" {\nnodesep=\"0.3\";\nranksep=\"0.3\";\nnode[fontsize=\"8\",fontname=\"Helvetica\",width=\"0.3\",height=\"0.2\"];\nedge[fontsize=\"8\",fontname=\"Helvetica\"];\n\n}\n";

20	/// Render the `intdot` mini HTML page.
21	///
22	/// * `theory_name` — used only in the `<title>` (not entity-escaped here since
23	///   the observed value is a plain identifier; escape upstream if needed).
24	/// * `dotsrc` — full path to the DOT source, e.g.
25	///   `/thy/trace/3/interactive-graph-def/proof/exec`.
26	pub fn render_intdot(theory_name: &str, dotsrc: &str) -> String {
27	    INTDOT_TEMPLATE
28	        .replace("§NAME§", theory_name)
29	        .replace("§DOTSRC§", dotsrc)
30	}

35	    format!("/thy/trace/{index}/interactive-graph-def/{trailing}")

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Live reference-server wrapper for UNIT A round 4 probing.
# Usage: wl_server4.sh start <file.spthy> <port> [extra tamarin flags...]
#        wl_server4.sh stop <port>
# Uses the --port=<n> (equals) form to avoid the workdir-positional bug.
export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
BIN="/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover"
cmd="$1"; shift
case "$cmd" in
  start)
    f="$1"; port="$2"; shift 2
    d=$(mktemp -d)
    cp "$f" "$d/"
    nohup "$BIN" interactive "$d" --port="$port" "$@" \
      >"/tmp/wl4_$port.log" 2>&1 &
    echo $! > "/tmp/wl4_$port.pid"
    sleep 5
    echo "up on $port (pid $(cat /tmp/wl4_$port.pid)) dir $d"
    ;;
  stop)
    port="$1"
    kill "$(cat /tmp/wl4_$port.pid)" 2>/dev/null
    rm -f "/tmp/wl4_$port.pid"
    echo "stopped $port"
    ;;
esac

# REPORT4.md — full route surface (the dispatcher owns the whole request path)

Round 4 of the `weblayer` clean-room cluster (Unit A, web dispatch). Rounds 1–3
reproduced the response bodies and the interactive read/proof/edit state machine.
This round extends `route::Route`/`route::Toplevel` and `Server<P: ProverOps>` to
the **rest** of the observed surface so the dispatcher owns the consumer's entire
request path.

Everything below is black-box only: live probing of the sanctioned oracle
([R40]–[R4B], ports 3140-3143, Tutorial/NSLPK3/KCL diff theory) plus a corpus key
census. No file under `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` was read; the only tamarin-rs
touch was executing the sanctioned binary (`interactive`, `--diff`, `--port=`).

## Surface added this round

* **Top level** (`route::Toplevel`): `GET /` (index) + `POST /` (theory upload),
  `/robots.txt`, `/favicon.ico` (→ static icon), `/kill` (cancel a search), and
  `/static/**` (filesystem assets), plus the `/thy/…` dispatch.
* **Theory scope**: `reload`, `download/{file}`, `get_and_append/{file}`, and the
  diff (`equiv`) analogues `diffProof` / `diffMethod` / `diffrules` /
  `autoproveDiff` / `autoproveAll`.
* **Error family**: the `404`/`400`/`405` Yesod default-layout pages, generalised
  from the round-1 single 404 template.

## Behaviour pinned down (see BEHAVIOR.md §14)

* **One global version-index namespace.** Proof ops **and uploads** each allocate
  a fresh index off the same monotonic counter; structural edits and **`reload`**
  mutate in place (counter untouched, other versions retained). Reload does not
  reset the counter or drop modified versions — verified by index probes after
  method → reload → method sequences ([R43], [R45]).
* **Envelopes.** A third JSON shape `{"alert":…}` (get_and_append; and a failed
  `method`/`diffMethod`, which does **not** bump the version). `download` = the
  `source` bytes with content type `application/octet-stream` and no
  `Content-Disposition`. `reload` = a JSON `{redirect}` (not a 303).
* **Redirect caching.** All `303`s (edit/add/delete success, delete-not-found,
  favicon) carry `Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate` + a fixed past
  `Expires` — modelled by `Response.no_cache`.
* **Static.** Content type by extension without `charset`
  (`text/css`/`application/javascript`/`image/png`/`image/vnd.microsoft.icon`/
  `application/octet-stream`); a miss is a plain-text `File not found`.
* **Delete branch** (refines §13.3): found → `303 overview/help`; not found →
  `303 overview/delete/{name}`. `delete_lemma` now returns `Option`.
* **Diff mode** (`--diff`, theory-kind `equiv`): the overview shell is the trace
  shell with a `DiffTheory:` title, `/thy/equiv/` links, and no "Append modified
  lemmas" Actions item; `diffMethod` redirects to `overview/diffProof/…`.

## Two documented negative results

* **`del/path` and `verify`** return `404` (not `405`) for every method/shape in
  both modes, and appear in **zero** manifest keys — neither in the corpus nor
  live-observable in oracle 1.13.0. Routed to `Handler::Other` (→ 404); no bytes
  fabricated. ([R47])
* **`autoproveDiff` / `autoproveAll`** block the prover on the probe theory
  (curl timeouts), so their exact redirect bytes are unobservable here. Parsed
  and dispatched as proof ops (new version) with the redirect modelled by analogy
  to `diffMethod`/`autoprove`. ([R49])

## Deliverables

* `src/route.rs` — `Toplevel` entry point; `Handler` gains
  `Reload`/`Download`/`GetAndAppend`/`Edit`/`AutoproveDiff`/`AutoproveAll`; `Main`
  gains `DiffProof`/`DiffMethod`/`DiffRules`; `AutoproveDiff`/`AutoproveAll`
  parsers; `OverviewView` gains `DiffProof`/`Delete`.
* `src/dispatch.rs` — `Server::dispatch` now takes the whole `Toplevel` surface;
  `Response` gains `no_cache`; `Request` gains `query`; `ProverOps` grown
  minimally (pure data producers + `Option`-returning `apply_method`/`delete_lemma`).
* `src/page.rs` + `src/shell_template.rs` — shell parameterised over
  `ShellKind` (trace/equiv); index-page (`render_root`/`render_root_row`) and the
  root/simple-page templates.
* `src/assets.rs` (new) — static content-type map + fixed text bodies.
* `src/errors.rs` — 404/400/405 from a shared simple-page template
  (`notfound_template.rs` removed).
* `src/envelope.rs` — the `{alert}` shape.
* `tests/dispatch4.rs` (17 tests) + `tests/fixtures/r4_*` — capture-backed
  byte-parity/behaviour tests; `BEHAVIOR.md` §14, `QUERIES.log` [R40]–[R4B].

`cargo test` → **75 passing** (24 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 19 parity),
`cargo clippy --tests --examples` clean. Byte-parity vs live captures for
`robots.txt`, the Invalid-Arguments (400) and Method-Not-Supported (405) pages,
`Canceled request!`, `download == source`, `reload` redirect, the index page
(decomposition), and the equiv overview shell (decomposition); all re-verified
byte-identical on a fresh server instance ([R4B]). Existing round-1..3 tests
unchanged in intent (the round-3 `FakeProver` was updated for the `Option`-typed
callbacks; its assertions still hold). All servers stopped.

## Honesty / scope notes

* `ProverOps` returns opaque fragments; tests feed canned fragments so the
  asserted bytes are the **web-layer** assembly (envelope/status/Location/version/
  content-type/cache), never re-derived prover logic.
* Index-page rows (load time, origin) and the upload/diff proof-state content are
  non-deterministic/prover fragments; the root page is byte-tested by
  decomposition with the exact captured row values fed as inputs.
* Static file **bytes** are on-disk environment assets: the model reproduces the
  content-type + 404 decision and passes a UTF-8 asset through exactly; binary
  passthrough is an integration-adapter concern (documented in `dispatch.rs`).
EOF

Round 4 of the `weblayer` clean-room cluster (Unit A, web dispatch). Rounds 1–3
reproduced the response bodies and the interactive read/proof/edit state machine.
This round extends `route::Route`/`route::Toplevel` and `Server<P: ProverOps>` to
the **rest** of the observed surface so the dispatcher owns the consumer's entire
request path.

Everything below is black-box only: live probing of the sanctioned oracle
([R40]–[R4B], ports 3140-3143, Tutorial/NSLPK3/KCL diff theory) plus a corpus key
census. No file under `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` was read; the only tamarin-rs
touch was executing the sanctioned binary (`interactive`, `--diff`, `--port=`).

## Surface added this round

* **Top level** (`route::Toplevel`): `GET /` (index) + `POST /` (theory upload),
  `/robots.txt`, `/favicon.ico` (→ static icon), `/kill` (cancel a search), and
  `/static/**` (filesystem assets), plus the `/thy/…` dispatch.
* **Theory scope**: `reload`, `download/{file}`, `get_and_append/{file}`, and the
  diff (`equiv`) analogues `diffProof` / `diffMethod` / `diffrules` /
  `autoproveDiff` / `autoproveAll`.
* **Error family**: the `404`/`400`/`405` Yesod default-layout pages, generalised
  from the round-1 single 404 template.

## Behaviour pinned down (see BEHAVIOR.md §14)

* **One global version-index namespace.** Proof ops **and uploads** each allocate
  a fresh index off the same monotonic counter; structural edits and **`reload`**
  mutate in place (counter untouched, other versions retained). Reload does not
  reset the counter or drop modified versions — verified by index probes after
  method → reload → method sequences ([R43], [R45]).
* **Envelopes.** A third JSON shape `{"alert":…}` (get_and_append; and a failed
  `method`/`diffMethod`, which does **not** bump the version). `download` = the
  `source` bytes with content type `application/octet-stream` and no
  `Content-Disposition`. `reload` = a JSON `{redirect}` (not a 303).
* **Redirect caching.** All `303`s (edit/add/delete success, delete-not-found,
  favicon) carry `Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate` + a fixed past
  `Expires` — modelled by `Response.no_cache`.
* **Static.** Content type by extension without `charset`
  (`text/css`/`application/javascript`/`image/png`/`image/vnd.microsoft.icon`/
  `application/octet-stream`); a miss is a plain-text `File not found`.
* **Delete branch** (refines §13.3): found → `303 overview/help`; not found →
  `303 overview/delete/{name}`. `delete_lemma` now returns `Option`.
* **Diff mode** (`--diff`, theory-kind `equiv`): the overview shell is the trace
  shell with a `DiffTheory:` title, `/thy/equiv/` links, and no "Append modified
  lemmas" Actions item; `diffMethod` redirects to `overview/diffProof/…`.

## Two documented negative results

* **`del/path` and `verify`** return `404` (not `405`) for every method/shape in
  both modes, and appear in **zero** manifest keys — neither in the corpus nor
  live-observable in oracle 1.13.0. Routed to `Handler::Other` (→ 404); no bytes
  fabricated. ([R47])
* **`autoproveDiff` / `autoproveAll`** block the prover on the probe theory
  (curl timeouts), so their exact redirect bytes are unobservable here. Parsed
  and dispatched as proof ops (new version) with the redirect modelled by analogy
  to `diffMethod`/`autoprove`. ([R49])

## Deliverables

* `src/route.rs` — `Toplevel` entry point; `Handler` gains
  `Reload`/`Download`/`GetAndAppend`/`Edit`/`AutoproveDiff`/`AutoproveAll`; `Main`
  gains `DiffProof`/`DiffMethod`/`DiffRules`; `AutoproveDiff`/`AutoproveAll`
  parsers; `OverviewView` gains `DiffProof`/`Delete`.
* `src/dispatch.rs` — `Server::dispatch` now takes the whole `Toplevel` surface;
  `Response` gains `no_cache`; `Request` gains `query`; `ProverOps` grown
  minimally (pure data producers + `Option`-returning `apply_method`/`delete_lemma`).
* `src/page.rs` + `src/shell_template.rs` — shell parameterised over
  `ShellKind` (trace/equiv); index-page (`render_root`/`render_root_row`) and the
  root/simple-page templates.
* `src/assets.rs` (new) — static content-type map + fixed text bodies.
* `src/errors.rs` — 404/400/405 from a shared simple-page template
  (`notfound_template.rs` removed).
* `src/envelope.rs` — the `{alert}` shape.
* `tests/dispatch4.rs` (17 tests) + `tests/fixtures/r4_*` — capture-backed
  byte-parity/behaviour tests; `BEHAVIOR.md` §14, `QUERIES.log` [R40]–[R4B].

`cargo test` → **75 passing** (24 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 19 parity),
`cargo clippy --tests --examples` clean. Byte-parity vs live captures for
`robots.txt`, the Invalid-Arguments (400) and Method-Not-Supported (405) pages,
`Canceled request!`, `download == source`, `reload` redirect, the index page
(decomposition), and the equiv overview shell (decomposition); all re-verified
byte-identical on a fresh server instance ([R4B]). Existing round-1..3 tests
unchanged in intent (the round-3 `FakeProver` was updated for the `Option`-typed
callbacks; its assertions still hold). All servers stopped.

## Honesty / scope notes

* `ProverOps` returns opaque fragments; tests feed canned fragments so the
  asserted bytes are the **web-layer** assembly (envelope/status/Location/version/
  content-type/cache), never re-derived prover logic.
* Index-page rows (load time, origin) and the upload/diff proof-state content are
  non-deterministic/prover fragments; the root page is byte-tested by
  decomposition with the exact captured row values fed as inputs.
* Static file **bytes** are on-disk environment assets: the model reproduces the
  content-type + 404 decision and passes a UTF-8 asset through exactly; binary
  passthrough is an integration-adapter concern (documented in `dispatch.rs`).

content.text:
All deliverables are in place, all servers stopped, and the full suite is green. Here is my round-4 summary.

UNIT A (web dispatch), round 4 — extended `Route`/`Toplevel` and `Server<P: ProverOps>` to own the consumer's ENTIRE request path. Black-box only (live oracle ports 3140-3143 on Tutorial/NSLPK3/KCL diff theory + corpus key census); no `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` file read, no web access.

New surface, each probed live for method/status/headers/body and byte-tested:
- `GET /` index and `POST /` upload (multipart `uploadedTheory`): upload returns the index page (200 HTML, not a redirect) with flash `Loaded new theory!`/`Post request failed.`, and allocates a fresh index off the SAME global monotonic counter as proof ops.
- `/robots.txt` (200 `text/plain` `User-agent: *`), `/favicon.ico` (303 → `/static/img/favicon.ico`, no-cache), `/kill` (GET-only; `?path=` → 200 `Canceled request!`, missing → 400 Invalid-Arguments page), `/static/**` (content-type by extension without charset; missing → plaintext `File not found`).
- Theory scope: `download/{file}` (octet-stream, no Content-Disposition, bytes == `source`), `reload` (POST → JSON `{redirect}` in place, retains other versions, counter NOT reset), `get_and_append` (new `{alert}` JSON shape), and the full diff/`equiv` surface (`DiffTheory:` shell, `diffProof`/`diffMethod`/`diffrules`, `autoproveDiff`/`autoproveAll`).

State machine: one global version-index namespace — proof ops and uploads bump a shared monotonic counter; structural edits and reload mutate in place. Refinements: delete found→303 `overview/help` / not-found→303 `overview/delete/{name}`; failed method/diffMethod→`{alert}` with no bump. All 303s carry `Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate` + fixed `Expires` (`Response.no_cache`). Error family (404/400/405) generalized to a shared simple-page template.

Two honest negative results: `del/path` and `verify` return 404 (not 405) for every method/shape in both modes and appear in zero manifest keys — absent in oracle 1.13.0, routed to `Handler::Other`, no bytes fabricated. `autoproveDiff`/`autoproveAll` block the prover on the probe theory, so their exact redirect bytes are unobservable — dispatched as proof ops with redirect modelled by analogy to `diffMethod`.

ProverOps grown minimally (pure data producers: `root_meta`, `append_message`, `static_file`, `load_theory`, `reload`, diff ops; `apply_method`/`delete_lemma` now return `Option`); all transport stays in `Server`. `cargo test` → 75 passing (24 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 19 parity), `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean; every round-1..3 test still green. Byte-parity re-verified on a fresh server instance. All servers stopped.

Key paths: `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/web-clean/src/{route.rs,dispatch.rs,page.rs,shell_template.rs,assets.rs,errors.rs,envelope.rs}`, tests `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/web-clean/tests/dispatch4.rs` (+ `tests/fixtures/r4_*`), docs `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/{BEHAVIOR.md (§14),QUERIES.log ([R40]-[R4B]),REPORT4.md}`.
